seoTitle: How to Print Your Tax Return in TurboTax Online
metaDescription: Get a physical or PDF copy of your completed TurboTax Online return for your records, a loan application, or business bookkeeping.
intro: Sometimes a digital tax return isn't enough — lenders, landlords, and your own filing cabinet often want a printed copy. Here's how to pull a printable version of your return out of TurboTax Online once it's finished, without second-guessing whether you grabbed the right pages.

steps.heading: Finish and review your return
steps.body: Make sure your return is complete before printing, since TurboTax Online generates the printable version from your final entered data.

steps.heading: Access your TurboTax Online account
steps.body: Sign in and open the tax return you want to print from your account dashboard.

steps.heading: Find the print option
steps.body: Navigate to the section of TurboTax Online where you can view or download your completed return as a document.

steps.heading: Check the document before printing
steps.body: Skim through the generated pages to confirm all your income, deductions, and forms are present and correct.

steps.heading: Print or save your copy
steps.body: Send the return to your printer or save it as a PDF for your records, whether that's for personal files, a loan application, or business documentation.

faq.question: Can I print my return before I finish filing?
faq.answer: You can generally view and print a copy at various stages, but it's best to wait until your return is complete so the printed version reflects your final numbers.

faq.question: Do I need to print my return every year?
faq.answer: It's not required for e-filing, but many people keep a printed or PDF copy for their own records, loan applications, or bookkeeping.

faq.question: Can small business owners use the printed return for documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, a printed copy can serve as part of your financial documentation and bookkeeping records alongside other business paperwork.

faq.question: What if my printed return looks incomplete?
faq.answer: Go back into your TurboTax Online account and confirm every section of your return was fully completed before generating the print version again.
